title: Okta Identity Integration Guide
content:
## Overview

Okta Identity Engine is a leading cloud-based identity and access management (IAM) platform developed by Okta, Inc. It provides centralized user lifecycle management, single sign-on (SSO), multi-factor authentication (MFA), and directory services. The T1 Agentics integration with Okta enables SOC teams to investigate user accounts, manage access during security incidents, enforce MFA, and retrieve audit logs directly from the T1 Agentics platform.

**Vendor:** Okta
**Category:** Identity & Access Management
**Authentication:** API Key (SSWS Token)

## Prerequisites

Before configuring the Okta Identity integration in T1 Agentics, ensure you have:

- An active Okta organization with administrative access
- An Okta API token (SSWS token) generated from the Okta Admin Console under **Security > API > Tokens**
- The service account or admin user generating the token must have appropriate permissions (Super Admin or a custom role with read/write access to Users, Groups, and Logs)
- Your Okta domain URL (e.g., `yourorg.okta.com`)
- Network access from the T1 Agentics platform to the Okta API endpoint (`https://{domain}.okta.com/api/v1`)
- Rate limit awareness: Okta enforces per-endpoint rate limits; ensure your token is not shared with other high-volume integrations

## Configuration in T1 Agentics

1. Navigate to **Connect > Marketplace** in the T1 Agentics sidebar
2. Search for **Okta Identity Engine** or browse the **Identity** category
3. Click on the Okta Identity Engine card and select **Configure**
4. Enter your connection details:
   - **Connection Name**: A descriptive label (e.g., "Okta Production")
   - **Domain**: Your Okta organization domain (e.g., `yourorg.okta.com`)
   - **API Key**: Paste your SSWS API token
5. Click **Test Connection** to verify the credentials are valid
6. Click **Save** to activate the integration
7. The integration will appear in your **Connect > Active Integrations** list

## Available Actions

| Action | Type | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| List Users | Investigate | List all users with optional filtering and search |
| Get User | Investigate | Retrieve details of a specific user by ID, login, or email |
| Disable User | Contain | Deactivate a user account, preventing all login and access |
| Enable User | Correct | Reactivate a previously deactivated user account |
| Suspend User | Contain | Temporarily suspend a user account |
| Unsuspend User | Correct | Restore a suspended user account |
| Reset Password | Contain | Generate a one-time password reset token for a user |
| Clear User Sessions | Contain | Revoke all active sessions, forcing re-authentication |
| List Groups | Investigate | List all groups in the Okta organization |
| Get Group | Investigate | Retrieve details of a specific group |
| List Group Members | Investigate | List all members of a specific group |
| Add User to Group | Generic | Add a user to a specified group |
| Remove User from Group | Contain | Remove a user from a specified group |
| List User MFA Factors | Investigate | List all enrolled MFA factors for a user |
| Get System Log Events | Investigate | Retrieve audit log events from the Okta system log |

## Common Use Cases

- **Compromised account containment**: When an account compromise is detected, immediately suspend the user, clear all active sessions, and reset their password to prevent further unauthorized access
- **Privilege escalation investigation**: Query group memberships and recent system log events to determine if a user was added to privileged groups unexpectedly
- **MFA compliance auditing**: List MFA factors for users across the organization to identify accounts that lack strong authentication methods
- **Automated incident response playbooks**: Integrate Okta actions into T1 Agentics playbooks to automatically disable accounts when correlated with threat intelligence or SIEM alerts
- **User activity forensics**: Pull Okta system log events for a specific timeframe to investigate suspicious login patterns, failed authentication attempts, or policy violations

## Troubleshooting

| Issue | Solution |
|-------|----------|
| 401 Unauthorized errors | Verify your API token is correct and has not been revoked. Regenerate the token from Okta Admin Console if needed |
| 403 Forbidden on certain actions | The API token's associated user lacks the required admin permissions. Ensure the user has Super Admin or a custom role with appropriate scopes |
| Rate limit (429) errors | Okta enforces per-endpoint rate limits. Reduce polling frequency or implement retry logic with exponential backoff |
| Connection timeout | Verify network connectivity from the T1 Agentics platform to your Okta domain. Check firewall rules and proxy settings |
| User not found errors | Ensure you are using the correct user identifier (Okta user ID, login email, or shortname). The identifier format varies by endpoint |
| System log returns empty results | Check the `since` and `until` date parameters. Okta system log retention depends on your Okta plan (typically 90 days for most plans) |
